message:
fix(policy,engine,mux,install): trauma-bypass detection, flat JumpTo schema, thread-safe env, cargo install args

Five targeted fixes:

1. policy.rs — Rewrite has_trauma_bypass_prefix() to actually detect
   FT_BYPASS_TRAUMA=1 instead of broadly flagging any command substitution
   in variable assignments. The old logic had false positives (any $() in
   assignments) and false negatives (never checked for the specific bypass
   variable). New code iterates assignments checking for FT_BYPASS_TRAUMA=1
   in bare, double-quoted, or single-quoted forms.

2. engine.rs — Flatten JumpTo result_data JSON schema from deeply nested
   { "step_result": { "JumpTo": { "step": N } } } to flat
   { "type": "jump_to", "step": N }. The old .pointer("/step_result/JumpTo/step")
   was unnecessarily complex; the new .get("step") is cleaner and consistent
   with the type-discriminated result format. Test updated to match.

3. mux-server/main.rs — Move unsafe set_var() call out of the listener
   spawn loop into a guarded block before the loop using config.unix_domains
   .first(). Calling set_var inside a loop that also spawns threads is a
   data race (set_var is not thread-safe and is unsafe in recent Rust
   editions). Now sets the env var exactly once before any threads exist.

4. install.sh — Fix cargo install invocation: add explicit package name
   `frankenterm` and --bin flag to select the specific binary. The old
   form passed the binary name as the positional arg, which cargo install
   expects to be a package name.

5. fix_deps.sh — Add || true to grep pipeline in get_id() for errexit
   safety. Without it, grep returning no match (exit 1) would abort the
   script under set -e.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@ br list --json | jq -r '.[] | "\(.id)|\(.title)"' > issues.tmp
 
 function get_id() {
     local title="$1"
-    grep "$title" issues.tmp | head -n 1 | cut -d'|' -f1
+    grep "$title" issues.tmp | head -n 1 | cut -d'|' -f1 || true
 }
 
 # Epic 1

@@ -48,11 +48,11 @@ install_ft() {
     log_info "Installing ft (frankenterm)..."
 
     # Use cargo install from git
-    if cargo install --git "$REPO_URL" "$BINARY_NAME" --locked; then
+    if cargo install --git "$REPO_URL" frankenterm --bin "$BINARY_NAME" --locked; then
         log_success "ft installed successfully"
     else
         log_warn "Locked install failed, trying without --locked"
-        cargo install --git "$REPO_URL" "$BINARY_NAME"
+        cargo install --git "$REPO_URL" frankenterm --bin "$BINARY_NAME"
     fi
 }
